stories.title: CPSC Recall — TOMY Boon NURSH 8 oz Baby Bottles (Choking Hazard, Walmart)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Boon NURSH Pink Tie Dye 8 oz bottles

Walmart, Nov 2025–May 2026. Plastic shell can peel — choking hazard.

Write "RECALL" on bottles + photo. Get replacement or $22 credit.

recall.tomy.com/nursh

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Do you have Boon NURSH bottles in your cabinet?

Check for item B11654 or UPC 669028116546. Pink Tie Dye 3-pack only. Sold at Walmart — ~40,000 recalled for choking hazard.

recall.tomy.com/nursh

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you have Boon NURSH baby bottles at home, check them right now.

TOMY International, in cooperation with the CPSC, recalled about 40,000 Boon NURSH Silicone Bottle 8 oz 3-Packs in Pink Tie Dye on June 4. The hard plastic outer shell can bubble or partially peel off, releasing loose pieces of film-like plastic that pose a choking hazard to babies.

The bottles were sold exclusively at Walmart and Walmart.com from November 2025 through May 2026 for about $20. To check if yours are affected, look at the bottom of the packaging for item number B11654 or UPC 669028116546. Only the Pink Tie Dye colorway is recalled — solid colors are not.

Here's how to get your refund or replacement: write the word "RECALL" on each bottle, photograph them, and submit the photos to certify they've been destroyed. You'll receive either a replacement 3-pack in a solid pink color or a $22 store credit at Walmart.com.

To start the process, visit recall.tomy.com/nursh or call 866-725-4407 (Monday–Friday, 8 AM–4 PM CT). You can also email cs@tomy.com. TOMY has received 135 reports of the peeling issue, and no injuries have been reported.

If you've seen something similar with your bottles, that's the issue. Don't wait on this one.

Do you use Boon bottles? Check yours today and share this with anyone who might have them.

---

article.headline: RECALL: TOMY Boon NURSH Baby Bottles Sold at Walmart Recalled for Choking Hazard

stories.title: Pikes Peak Gem, Mineral & Jewelry Show — TODAY Through Sunday at Norris Penrose

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Pikes Peak Gem, Mineral & Jewelry Show — this weekend in COS.

Norris Penrose Event Center, today–Sunday. Kids 12 & under FREE. $5 adults.

Gold panning, scavenger hunt, Pebble Pups, 55+ vendors.

pikespeakgemshow.com

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Pikes Peak Gem Show reminder: still open today until 6 PM at Norris Penrose.

Kids 12 & under get in FREE. Beading class at 11 AM daily. Wire-wrapping class Saturday at 1 PM.

Cash appreciated at the door.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Looking for something fun to do with the kids this weekend in Colorado Springs? It doesn't get more affordable than today.

The Pikes Peak Gem, Mineral & Jewelry Show is happening right now through Sunday at the Norris Penrose Event Center (1045 Lower Gold Camp Rd), and kids 12 and under get in FREE. Adults are only $5 for a one-day pass or $8 for the whole weekend.

The Colorado Springs Mineralogical Society hosts this annual show with more than 55 vendors selling gems, minerals, fossils, meteorites, crystals, and handmade jewelry. For families with curious kids, here are the highlights:

- Gold panning
- Scavenger hunt through the vendor floor
- Pebble Pups group (perfect for junior rock collectors)
- Beading classes daily at 11 AM
- Wire-wrapping class Saturday at 1 PM (great parent-kid activity)

Hours are Friday and Saturday 10 AM–6 PM and Sunday 10 AM–4 PM. Tickets are sold at the door only — cash is appreciated. No registration needed, just show up.

With summer break in full swing, this is a great low-cost outing that combines education, creativity, and a little treasure hunting.

Have your kids ever gone gold panning? Tell us in the comments.

---

article.headline: Pikes Peak Gem, Mineral & Jewelry Show This Weekend: Kids Under 12 Free, Gold Panning and More

stories.title: D20 Families — Transportation Deadline June 30, New Students Apply Starting June 8

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
D20: transportation requests for 2026–27 due June 30.

New students: open June 8. Confirm address in Infinite Campus. Bus assignments Aug 1.

Budget public hearing: June 11, 5 PM, 1110 Chapel Hills Dr.

asd20.org

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D20 families, two important dates to put on your calendar this month.

First: transportation requests. If you'll need bus service for the 2026–27 school year, the deadline to submit your request is June 30. If you're a new D20 student, requests open June 8. If you've been in the district before, the window has been open since May 1.

Before you submit, make sure your address is updated and confirmed in Infinite Campus, and that any applicable transportation fees are paid. Bus assignments won't be posted until August 1 through the Extended Parent Portal — so the earlier you submit, the better. Full details at asd20.org/buses-and-transportation.

Second: the 2026–27 budget. Academy District 20 is holding a public budget hearing on June 11 during the Board of Education meeting at 1110 Chapel Hills Drive. The Study Session starts at 3:30 PM and the full Board Meeting begins at 5 PM. The district is required to adopt a budget by June 30, and public input is part of that process.

The district is currently operating under Interim Superintendent Dr. Susan Field, who stepped in after the board parted ways with Superintendent Jinger Haberer in May.

Are you navigating D20 transportation or budget changes this year? Let us know in the comments.

---

stories.title: New National Survey — 72% of Parents Want More Time With Their Kids But Money Gets in the Way

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text:
New survey: 72% of parents want more time with their kids.

The No. 1 barrier? Money. U.S. child care averages $13,184 per child per year.

COS parents are living this data every day.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text:
2026 National Parent Survey: parental leave medians are 10 weeks for moms, 2 weeks for dads.

Most said that wasn't enough.

91% of parents want to work — they just want more flexibility and support.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
New survey results just dropped — and if you've ever felt pulled in 10 directions at once, the data says you're not alone.

The 2026 National Parent Survey from New America's New Practice Lab surveyed 5,472 parents from every state, and the results are striking. Nearly three in four parents — 72% — say they want more quality time with their kids. But here's the catch: money is the biggest thing in the way.

The average cost of child care in the US is now $13,184 per child per year — that's about one-quarter of a family's income for households at 200% of the federal poverty level. At the same time, 75% of parents say their current work arrangement isn't what they want. It's not that families don't want to be home — they're navigating a financial reality that doesn't make it easy.

On parental leave, the median is 10 weeks for mothers and 2 weeks for fathers. A majority of both said that wasn't enough time.

What's interesting is that 91% of parents said they do want to work — they just want more flexibility, higher wages, and enough breathing room to actually be present when they're home.

For Colorado Springs families, none of this is surprising. COS has about 47,000 children under 5 and fewer than 19,000 licensed childcare spots. The national survey puts data behind something local parents already feel.

What would genuinely help your family find more quality time together?

---

article.headline: 72% of U.S. Parents Want More Time With Their Kids — New Survey Shows Money Is the Barrier

stories.title: COS Summer Sun Safety Guide — Altitude Means Higher UV Risk for Kids

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
COS sits at 6,035 feet — UV is ~24% stronger than at sea level.

June/July UV index hits 8–10 (Very High to Extreme). Plan outdoor time before 10 AM or after 4 PM. SPF 30+, reapply every 2 hrs.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Now that summer is officially here, here's a gentle reminder that Colorado Springs isn't like most places when it comes to sun safety.

At 6,035 feet above sea level, Colorado Springs receives significantly more UV radiation than coastal or lower-elevation cities — roughly 24% more than at sea level. During June and July, the UV index here regularly hits 8 or above, which the EPA classifies as "Very High," and can spike to 10 or higher ("Extreme") on clear summer days.

What that means practically for COS families spending time outdoors:

**Sunscreen:** Use SPF 30 or higher. Apply it 15 minutes before heading outside and reapply every two hours — more often after swimming or sweating. Spray sunscreens need to be rubbed in to work.

**Timing:** The sun is strongest between 10 AM and 4 PM. If you're planning outdoor activities — a trip to the park, Get Outdoors Day at Memorial Park, hiking — early morning or late afternoon is significantly safer for kids' skin.

**Babies:** The American Academy of Pediatrics recommends keeping infants under six months out of direct sunlight entirely. Shade, protective clothing, and hats are the primary protection for the youngest members of your family. Sunscreen is generally not recommended for infants under six months.

**Heat and hydration:** Children, especially toddlers, dehydrate faster than adults. On warm days, offer water frequently. Signs of heat exhaustion include heavy sweating, pale or cool skin, weakness, dizziness, and nausea. If any of these appear, move to shade, give fluids, and call your pediatrician if symptoms don't improve quickly.

**Protective clothing:** A wide-brimmed hat and UV-protective clothing (look for UPF 50+ labels) add meaningful protection on full-sun days.

COS summers are beautiful. A few habits now make the whole season easier for your family.

What are your go-to sun safety products for COS summer?

---

article.headline: Summer Sun Safety in Colorado Springs: What Parents Need to Know About High-Altitude UV Risk
